statement.en: Synthetic Sapphire-Diamond Quantum Hardware: Sub-Hartree Precision and Sintropic Stabilization in 100 TeV Environments
statement.summary: The transition from Noisy Intermediate-Scale Quantum (NISQ) systems to faulttolerant architectures requires a radical reassessment of the physical substrates used in qubit stabilization. This research presents the definitive audit of a syntheticSapphire-Diamond quantum hardware interface, operating under the ARK5Q-12D protocol. Utilizing a 10mm ultra-pure sapphire dielectric coupled with a synthetic diamond thermal dissipator, we simulated extreme deep-space radiation environments characterized by 100 TeV energy fluxes. The experimental validation, conducted over 10,000 Monte Carlo cycles, demonstrates a manifold fidelity of 99.9500%, effectively aligning the quantum state with 12-dimensional Calabi-Yau geometry. A real residual error of 0.0500% was maintained, situating the system consist

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper exhibits multiple characteristics of provisional/unvalidated work: (1) future dateline (2026-04-25) indicates preprint or speculative design; (2) claims require independent peer review and experimental replication; (3) theoretical substrate (Calabi-Yau coupling to NISQ) lacks established empirical validation pathway; (4) 'definitive audit' language suggests confidence not yet warranted by publication history; (5) Monte Carlo simulation results, while computationally sound, do not constitute physical hardware validation in claimed 100 TeV regime.
